sample.Insight: Small but extremely valuable group that drives most future revenue.
sample.Recommendation: Protect with loyalty rewards, VIP campaigns, early access, and personalized recommendations.

sample.Insight: Largest stable customer group with moderate revenue potential.
sample.Recommendation: Grow using cross-sell offers, bundles, category recommendations, and moderate-value campaigns.

sample.Insight: Previously active customers whose long recency makes them risky.
sample.Recommendation: Reactivate using win-back discounts, reminder campaigns, and limited-time offers.

sample.insight: The calibrated XGBoost Tweedie CLV model is the strongest predictive component of the project.
sample.businessMeaning: The business can prioritize customers by expected future value instead of treating all retained customers equally.

sample.insight: The top predicted CLV customers captured a disproportionate share of actual future revenue.
sample.businessMeaning: Revenue is concentrated among a small group of high-value customers, making targeted campaigns more efficient.

sample.insight: KMeans produced four interpretable customer groups.
sample.businessMeaning: The customer base can be separated into loyal high-value customers, regular mid-value customers, inactive customers, and new/one-time customers.

sample.title: Final CLV Model
sample.description: Selected model for predicting customer future spend.

sample.title: CLV R²
sample.description: Explained variance for customer future spend prediction.

sample.title: CLV RMSE
sample.description: Average large-error scale for customer spend prediction.

sample.Main Insight: Customer-level future spend can be predicted well enough for revenue prioritization.
A small high-value segment contributes a large share of future revenue.
Forecasting is useful as short-term directional revenue planning, not production-grade long-term forecasting.

sample.Recommendation: Protect with loyalty rewards, VIP campaigns, early access, and personalized recommendations.
